description: Single coherent Chromium integration patch for Project Seoul native core V1. Materialize native/seoul/ first, then apply. Wires organization and lifecycle circular-implementation targets into //chrome/browser and registers the profile-keyed factory once.

entrySchema.rationale: why it is necessary and which upstream alternative was rejected
entrySchema.baseRevision: full Chromium SHA this patch is authored against; must equal manifest baseRevision
entrySchema.order: unique ascending integer giving application order
entrySchema.file: patch file path relative to patchDir
affectedPaths: upstream src-relative paths the patch touches
entrySchema.sha256: lowercase hex sha256 of the patch file
entrySchema.applyVerify: command/strategy proving the patch applies cleanly (git apply --check)
entrySchema.reverseVerify: command/strategy proving the patch reverses cleanly (git apply -R --check)
entrySchema.reviewable: true once the patch is independently reviewable in isolation

patches.description: Wire Seoul organization, lifecycle, command, projection, shell, product-runtime, and canvas targets into //chrome/browser (deps + allow_circular_includes_from); register SeoulOrganizationServiceFactory and SeoulRuntimeServiceFactory once at profile-service init; register Seoul projection and shell against VerticalTabStripRegionView only AFTER RootTabCollectionNode::Init() and unregister them at the start of ResetTabStrip; expose a production GetSeoulRootNode() accessor (no testing-only accessor in production); forward collapse-state changes to the shell; register the SeoulCanvasUIConfig (chrome://seoul-canvas) in the WebUI config map and package its resources (resource_ids.spec range plus chrome/browser/resources pack dep); add the kSeoulCanvas side-panel entry id; and wire the Seoul in-process browser-test source_sets (commands, projection, shell) into the //chrome/test:browser_tests target so they build and run under the standard browser_tests binary.
patches.rationale: Chromium-facing Seoul glue includes //chrome/browser headers and must use the browser_public_dependencies circular-implementation pattern. A single patch avoids duplicate lifecycle linkage (organization already depends on lifecycle transitively) and keeps factory registration in one place.
